creator: mark@foster.cc
text:
Just did a portsnap fetch && portsnap extract.
portmaster p5-libwww yields a recursive loop on p5-libwww
as does the make command in /usr/ports/www/p5-libwww
It does not seem to matter whether HTTPS support is enabled or not.

dogma# cat /var/db/ports/p5-libwww/options
# This file is auto-generated by 'make config'.
# Options for p5-libwww-6.04
_OPTIONS_READ=p5-libwww-6.04
_FILE_COMPLETE_OPTIONS_LIST=HTTPS
OPTIONS_FILE_SET+=HTTPS

dogma# rm /var/db/ports/p5-libwww/options

dogma# portmaster p5-libwww

===>>> Gathering dependency list for www/p5-libwww from ports
===>>> Launching child to install converters/p5-Encode-Locale
        p5-libwww-5.837 >> converters/p5-Encode-Locale

===>>> Port directory: /usr/ports/converters/p5-Encode-Locale

===>>> Launching 'make checksum' for converters/p5-Encode-Locale in
background
===> No options to configure

===>>> Initial dependency check complete for converters/p5-Encode-Locale
        p5-libwww-5.837 >> converters/p5-Encode-Locale
===>>> Continuing initial dependency check for www/p5-libwww

===>>> The dependency for net/p5-Net-HTTP
       seems to be handled by p5-libwww-5.837

===>>> Launching child to update p5-libwww-5.837 to p5-libwww-6.04
        p5-libwww-5.837 >> p5-libwww-5.837

Note: uncancelled, it eventually looks like this in an endless loop:

Fix: 

Unknown
